content.problemStatement:
According to the Premier League rules, the winner of a game gets 333 points while the loser of a game gets 000 points. On the other hand, if there is a draw, both sides get 111 point each.

Two teams AAA and BBB played several games against each other and finally ended up with the score of XXX and YYY points respectively.

We do not know how many games were played. Find the **minimum** possible number of draws that may have happened.

It is guaranteed that there is at least one way to end up with the final score being XXX and YYY points respectively.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case contains XXX and YYY - the final scores of teams AAA and BBB respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum number of draws that could have happened.
constraints: There is at least 111 way to obtain the final score XXX and YYY.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : Both teams played just 111 game and it was a draw. Thus, there was 111 draw.
Test case 222 : Both teams played just 111 game and team AAA won it. Thus, there were no draws at all.
